title: Tallyguard configuration
description: Repo-level configuration for the Tallyguard static analyzer. See docs/specs/SUPPRESSION-AND-FALSE-POSITIVES.md.

$schema.description: Pointer to this schema for editor completion.

version.description: Config schema version. Bumped only on a breaking change to this shape.

include.description: Globs of files to scan. Defaults to the analyzer's built-in set when omitted.

exclude.description: Globs of files to skip. Applied after include.

rules.description: Per-rule level. Unlisted rules use their default severity.

handledAtEdge.description: When true, globally suppress Detector 1: the team declares a gateway/WAF/platform limiter handles rate limiting.

edgeHandledPaths.description: Route globs where rate limiting is handled at the edge. Scopes the declaration instead of suppressing globally.

unknownGuard.description: Behavior when a route is guarded by a middleware the catalogue does not recognize. 'flag' (default) flags with an informative message naming the guard so the developer can verify, catalogue, or suppress it; 'suppress' assumes it may be protective and does not flag (still recorded in the suppressed output).

maxDepth.description: How many levels of function calls to follow from a handler into helper functions.

requireReason.description: When true (default), a suppression without a ' -- <reason>' is reported as tallyguard/suppression-without-reason. Set false to relax.

allowBlanket.description: When false, a suppression comment that omits the rule ID (suppressing all rules) is rejected.

ruleLevel.description: error trips a findings exit code; warn reports without tripping it; off disables the rule.
